name: testing-and-ha
description: Selenium Grid, k6, MailHog, Chaos Mesh, HA pass across all charts, GitLab CI vendor builds.
sprintGoal: Harden all platform Helm charts with HA primitives (PDB, anti-affinity, replica minima) so every deployment survives a node failure.

stories.title: HA helpers template and foundation chart hardening (cilium, cert-manager, crossplane, sealed-secrets, vault, rook-ceph)
stories.description: Create charts/_globals/templates/_ha-helpers.tpl with shared Helm template helpers for PodDisruptionBudget and podAntiAffinity to avoid duplication across all charts. Then apply HA standards to the 6 foundation/infrastructure charts: cilium (DaemonSet — skip replicaCount, verify resource limits), cert-manager, crossplane, sealed-secrets, vault (openbao), rook-ceph (StatefulSet — verify volumeClaimTemplates use global.storageClass). For each non-DaemonSet chart: (1) set replicaCount >= 2 in values.yaml (3 for vault); (2) add PodDisruptionBudget using the new helper; (3) add podAntiAffinity using the new helper; (4) verify readinessProbe, livenessProbe, resources.requests, resources.limits on every container.
acceptanceCriteria: charts/_globals/templates/_ha-helpers.tpl exists with a PDB helper and an anti-affinity helper
helm lint charts/_globals/ passes with 0 errors
charts/cilium/ has resource limits on every container (DaemonSet — no replicaCount change)
charts/cert-manager/values.yaml has replicaCount >= 2; helm template | grep PodDisruptionBudget exits 0
charts/crossplane/values.yaml has replicaCount >= 2; helm template | grep PodDisruptionBudget exits 0
charts/sealed-secrets/values.yaml has replicaCount >= 2; helm template | grep PodDisruptionBudget exits 0
charts/vault/values.yaml has replicaCount >= 3; helm template | grep PodDisruptionBudget exits 0
charts/rook-ceph/ has PodDisruptionBudget; volumeClaimTemplates reference global.storageClass
helm lint passes on all 6 charts (exit 0)
helm template charts/<name>/ | grep podAntiAffinity exits 0 for each non-DaemonSet chart
stories.testPlan: helm lint charts/_globals/; for chart in cert-manager crossplane sealed-secrets vault rook-ceph; do helm lint charts/$chart/; helm template charts/$chart/ | grep PodDisruptionBudget || echo MISSING-PDB in $chart; helm template charts/$chart/ | grep podAntiAffinity || echo MISSING-AFFINITY in $chart; done; helm template charts/cilium/ | grep -A5 resources:
reviewNotes: [Review 1] AC failed: 'charts/cilium/ has resource limits on every container (DaemonSet — no replicaCount change)'. Command: helm template charts/cilium/ | python3 check-limits.py Output: MISSING LIMITS: container 'install-cni-binaries' — the install-cni-binaries initContainer has resources.requests (cpu: 100m, memory: 10Mi) but no resources.limits. The values.yaml has initResources.limits but the upstream Cilium chart does not apply initResources to this specific initContainer. Fix: add explicit resources with limits for install-cni-binaries in the cilium values.yaml using the correct upstream value key (e.g., cni.resources or installCNIBinariesResources).

[Review 1] AC failed: 'charts/rook-ceph/ has PodDisruptionBudget; volumeClaimTemplates reference global.storageClass'. PDB: PASS (kind: PodDisruptionBudget found in helm template output). volumeClaimTemplates reference global.storageClass: FAIL. Command: grep -rn 'global.storageClass' charts/rook-ceph/templates/ — returned no output. The rook-ceph templates directory contains only: _helpers.tpl, grafana-dashboard.yaml, jobs/verify-osds.yaml, poddisruptionbudget.yaml. None contain volumeClaimTemplates or references to .Values.global.storageClass. The storageClass references in values.yaml (lines 66, 92, 124) are for Ceph StorageClass objects (what Ceph creates), not for the upstream rook-ceph chart's own StatefulSet volumeClaimTemplates. Fix: either (a) add a CephCluster CR template that references {{ .Values.global.storageClass }} for mon/mgr volumeClaimTemplates, or (b) document that rook-ceph as a storage provider does not consume a StorageClass and remove/update the AC.
stories.returnedReason: Two ACs failed review: (1) Cilium install-cni-binaries initContainer missing resource limits — the correct upstream values.yaml key for this initContainer must be identified (e.g. cni.resources or installCNIBinariesResources) and added to charts/cilium/values.yaml. (2) rook-ceph volumeClaimTemplates AC is architecturally incorrect — rook-ceph creates StorageClasses, it does not consume them; either add a CephCluster CR template referencing global.storageClass or replace the AC with a documented exception.

smart.notes: Achievable score 3: creating 1 helper template + hardening 6 charts (values.yaml replicaCount, PDB, anti-affinity, probes, resource limits on each) is near the 3-point story budget ceiling for a single Ralph iteration — but bounded and well-specified.
